# tosijs v1.6.2

Path-based state management for web apps — proxy-based observers, no JSX, no virtual DOM, ~15kB gzipped.

- Docs: https://tosijs.net
- Source: https://github.com/tonioloewald/tosijs
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/tosijs

## Documentation

Each component's full documentation (with live code examples) is
embedded in its distributed JS file as inline comments. Read the
relevant dist/*.js file for complete docs, usage examples, and API.

## Components and Utilities

- dist/bind.js — Binding
  `bind()` lets you synchronize data / application state to the user-interface reliably,
- dist/list-binding.js — binding arrays
  The most likely source of complexity and performance issues in applications is
- dist/bindings.js — bindings
  `bindings` is simply a collection of common bindings.
- dist/blueprint-loader.js — blueprints
  One issue with standard web-components built with tosijs is that building them
- dist/color.js — color
  `tosijs` includes a lightweight, powerful `Color` class for manipulating colors.
- dist/elements.js — Creating Elements
  `tosijs` provides `elements` for easily and efficiently generating DOM elements
- dist/css.js — CSS
  `tosijs` provides a collection of utilities for working with CSS rules that
- dist/hot-reload.js — hotReload
  `hotReload()` persists any root-level paths in `xin` that its test function evaluates as true
- dist/make-component.js — makeComponent
  `makeComponent(tag: string, bluePrint: XinBlueprint<T>): Promise<XinComponentSpec<T>>`
- dist/metadata.js — metadata
  `tosiAccessor` returns the collision-free accessor object from any boxed proxy,
- dist/more-math.js — more-math
  Some simple functions egregiously missing from the Javascript `Math`
- dist/path-listener.js — path-listener
  `path-listener` implements the `tosijs` observer model. Although these events
- dist/share.js — share
  `share()` synchronizes state across browser tabs and windows. Pass it
- dist/sync.js — sync
  `sync()` synchronizes state across the network in real-time. Pass it a
- dist/xin.js — The tosi proxy
  `tosi()` assigns an object passed to it to a global state object,
- dist/throttle.js — throttle & debounce
  Usage:
- dist/xin-proxy.js — tosi, xin, and xinProxy
  The key to managing application state with `tosijs` are its **observer** proxies, `tosi` and `xin`. These are documen...
- dist/form-validation.js — Validation
  Form validation for custom elements using `ElementInternals`.
- dist/component.js — Web-Components
  easier.
